member.name: Chris Elmore

addresses: Caroline Court, 33 Caroline Street, Bridgend, CF31 1DW

member.synopsis: Chris Elmore is the Labour MP for Bridgend , and has been an MP continually since 5 May 2016. He currently holds the Government post of Parliamentary Under-Secretary (Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Wines: Government Assistance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps he plans to take to support the wine industry after February 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has supported the wine industry with duty freezes at 6 of the last 12 fiscal events, including the decision at Spring Budget 2024 to freeze alcohol duty until 1 February 2025.As part of the new alcohol duty reforms, the Government has remov...

writtenQuestions.heading: Wines: Excise Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of extending the easement for levying wine duty beyond February 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has supported the wine industry with duty freezes at 6 of the last 12 fiscal events, including the decision at Spring Budget 2024 to freeze alcohol duty until 1 February 2025.As part of the new alcohol duty reforms, the Government has remov...

writtenQuestions.heading: Music
writtenQuestions.questionText: What recent assessment she has made of the impact of the UK's departure from the EU on the music industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government continues to support the industry’s adaptation to new requirements, including through our Export Support Service and comprehensive GOV.UK guidance.Visa and permit free touring routes are available in 24 of 27 Member States including key mar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Online Safety Act 2023: Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Attorney General, how many prosecutions have been made under new criminal offences created by the Online Safety Act 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I wrote to you on this important subject on Friday 17 May 2024. For completeness, I set out my full written response below. Tackling violence that disproportionately impacts women and girls remains one of this government’s top priorities. Women and girls ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Relations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many bilateral meetings the Prime Minister has held with foreign leaders in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answer of 15 November 2021, Official Report, PQ 66179.

writtenQuestions.heading: Young Offender Institutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many times Nico 9 stun grenades were used in each youth offender institution in each year since 2017.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMPPS uses Nico 9 Stun grenades as a distraction device during planned interventions as part of an agreed tactical plan. They can only be employed by National Tactical Response Group (NTRG) staff who are highly trained in their use. The table below states...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners on Remand
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the total number of remand prisoners was in each prison on HMPPS estate as of 1 January 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice holds data on the number of remand prisoners in each prison in the HMPPS estate. This information is routinely published in the Offender Management Statistics Quarterly (OMSQ) Prison Population Data Tool (latest file here). The dat...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners on Remand
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the average time spent on remand was, broken down by prisoner (a) gender, (b) ethnicity, (c) age and (d) status as a Foreign National Offender in 2023.

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to support research-intensive companies in the battery sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Last year we announced a record £211m funding uplift for the Faraday Battery Challenge, which began in 2017 and supports world-class scientific technology development and manufacturing scale-up capability for batteries in the UK. This new funding brings t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Alternative Fuel Payments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether people without a direct relationship with their electricity supplier will be paid the Alternative Fuel Payment in February 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Households that need to apply for the Alternative Fuel Payment because they do not have a relationship with an electricity supplier will be able to do so through a GOV.UK portal. This portal will include an overview of eligibility and what applicants need...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy Bills Rebate: Applications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what his planned timetable is for the implementation of a portal for people who do not have a direct relationship with an electricity supplier to claim the Alternative Fuel Payment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Households that need to apply for the Alternative Fuel Payment because they do not have a relationship with an electricity supplier will be able to do so through a GOV.UK portal. This portal will include an overview of eligibility and what applicants need...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
